name: onvifscan
description: ONVIF device security scanner for testing authentication and brute-forcing credentials. Use when you need to assess security of IP cameras or ONVIF-enabled devices.
capabilities: Test ONVIF endpoints for unauthenticated access
Perform credential brute-forcing with customizable wordlists
Scan IP cameras and ONVIF-enabled IoT devices
Support verbose and quiet output formats
Rate-limited brute-forcing to prevent device overload
author: BrownFineSecurity
securitySummary: All 57 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a legitimate prompt wrapper skill for ONVIF security auditing. Pattern matches for Metasploit, C2 keywords, and weak crypto are triggered by documentation text and repository names, not actual malicious code. The skill is a markdown documentation file that provides structured access to the onvifscan binary for authorized security assessments. Built-in safeguards include rate limiting (20 attempts max) and warnings about potentially destructive endpoints.
valueStatement: Assessing the security of IP cameras and ONVIF-enabled devices is critical for protecting surveillance infrastructure. This skill provides structured access to authentication testing and credential brute-forcing tools for authorized security assessments.
userTitle: Scan ONVIF Devices for Security Vulnerabilities
limitations: Requires onvifscan binary installed in system path
Built-in wordlists may be limited for comprehensive testing
Cannot test devices without network connectivity

useCases.title: Assess ONVIF Device Security
useCases.description: Evaluate authentication mechanisms and discover vulnerabilities in IP camera firmware and configurations.

useCases.title: Audit Internal Surveillance Systems
useCases.description: Verify that organizational IP cameras meet security standards and have proper access controls configured.

useCases.title: Test IoT Device Vulnerabilities
useCases.description: Include ONVIF device assessment in red team engagements to identify weak credentials and misconfigurations.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Auth Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run an authentication scan on the ONVIF device at http://192.168.1.100 using onvifscan auth

promptTemplates.title: Verbose Security Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a verbose ONVIF security scan with full XML output on device 192.168.1.50

promptTemplates.title: Brute Force Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Attempt credential brute-forcing on the ONVIF device using the built-in wordlists

promptTemplates.title: Custom Wordlist Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run brute force against the target device using custom wordlists located at /path/to/usernames.txt and /path/to/passwords.txt

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before scanning any device or network
Use the auth scan mode first to understand device security posture
Review rate limits and avoid excessive brute-forcing attempts
antiPatterns: Running brute-forcing scans without explicit authorization
Testing production devices during business hours without coordination
Ignoring the warning about the -a flag testing destructive endpoints

faq.question: What ONVIF devices are supported?
faq.answer: Any device implementing the ONVIF protocol including IP cameras, NVRs, and video encoders from major manufacturers.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for brute-forcing?
faq.answer: The tool limits brute-forcing to 20 attempts by default to prevent device overload.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my security workflow?
faq.answer: Use the JSON output format for machine parsing and export findings to your reporting system.

faq.question: Is my scan data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: The skill is a local prompt wrapper. All scan data remains on your system.

faq.question: The scan failed with a connection error. What should I check?
faq.answer: Verify the device is reachable, confirm ONVIF service is running, and check firewall rules.

faq.question: How does this compare to other ONVIF testing tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-assisted guidance for the onvifscan tool with rate limiting and safety warnings.
